rawText:
My journey to AI actually started surprisingly with games and specifically chess. I started my professional career in game design, working for Bullfrog as a teenager, and decided at around 16 years old that AI would be my career after seeing how people enjoyed interacting with game AI.

At DeepMind, we used games as a development platform for AI research. Games provided the perfect testing ground -- they have clear rules, measurable outcomes, and increasing levels of complexity.

One of our landmark achievements was AlphaGo, which defeated world champion Lee Sedol in the ancient game of Go. The significance of this achievement cannot be overstated -- Go has more possible board positions than there are atoms in the universe, making brute-force computation impossible.

The key moment came with Move 37 in Game 2, a move that no human player would have considered but that turned out to be brilliant. This demonstrated that neural networks could discover strategies that go beyond human intuition, efficiently navigating complex decision trees in ways that surprised even the greatest experts.

Straight after AlphaGo, I felt ready to tackle scientific problems, and protein folding was the challenge I had been thinking about for almost 20 years. The protein folding problem -- predicting a protein's three-dimensional structure from its amino acid sequence -- had been one of the grand challenges of biology for over 50 years. Proteins are the molecular machines of life, responsible for virtually every function in every living cell. Understanding their structure is essential to understanding how they work, which in turn is essential for developing new medicines and understanding disease.

We presented AlphaFold2 in 2020, and it represented a fundamental breakthrough. The system could predict protein structures with accuracy comparable to experimental methods, but in minutes rather than the months or years required by traditional techniques like X-ray crystallography or cryo-electron microscopy. AlphaFold has since been used by more than two million researchers worldwide for work ranging from enzyme design to drug discovery. We released predicted structures for virtually all 200 million known proteins, creating a comprehensive database that has transformed the field of structural biology.

The success of AlphaFold demonstrates the enormous potential of AI as a tool for scientific discovery. By combining deep learning with scientific domain knowledge, we can accelerate research across many fields -- from materials science to drug development. At Isomorphic Labs, we are now applying these same principles to drug discovery, working to design new medicines at digital speed. The goal is to use AI not just to understand existing biological structures, but to design entirely new molecules with specific therapeutic properties. The age of digital biology is here, and AI will be the engine that drives the next era of scientific breakthroughs.

overview.title: Accelerating Scientific Discovery with AI — Nobel Prize Lecture
overview.source: Nobel Prize in Chemistry 2024 — Demis Hassabis

sentences.original: My journey to AI actually started surprisingly with games and specifically chess.
sentences.translation: 제가 AI에 이르게 된 여정은 놀랍게도 게임, 구체적으로는 체스에서 시작되었습니다.

vocabulary.example: His journey to becoming a doctor was long and difficult.
The test was surprisingly easy.
I was referring specifically to the first chapter.

expressions.usage: 자신의 경력이나 성취에 이른 과정을 소개할 때
일반적 진술을 좁혀 특정 사례를 강조할 때

patterns.pattern: My journey to [X] started with [Y]
patterns.explanation: 자신의 경력 또는 업적의 출발점을 소개하는 전형적 강연 오프닝 패턴.
examples: My journey to medicine started with a childhood illness.
My journey to entrepreneurship started with a simple idea.

drillQuestions.question: My journey _____ AI started with games.
It started _____ with games, not research.

sentences.notes: "actually"와 "surprisingly"가 함께 사용되어, 청중의 기대를 의도적으로 뒤엎는 효과를 준다. AI 연구자가 게임에서 출발했다는 의외성을 강조.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 의외성(surprise opening) — 노벨 화학상 수상 강연을 게임과 체스 이야기로 시작하여 청중의 호기심을 유발

sentences.original: I started my professional career in game design, working for Bullfrog as a teenager, and decided at around 16 years old that AI would be my career after seeing how people enjoyed interacting with game AI.
sentences.translation: 저는 10대 때 불프로그에서 일하며 게임 디자인으로 전문 경력을 시작했고, 사람들이 게임 AI와 상호작용하는 것을 즐기는 모습을 보고 16세 무렵 AI를 제 커리어로 삼기로 결심했습니다.

grammarElements.text: my professional career in game design
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: working for Bullfrog as a teenager
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (동시 상황)

grammarElements.text: that AI would be my career
grammarElements.label: that절 (목적절)

grammarElements.text: after seeing how people enjoyed interacting with game AI
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (이유/시간)

vocabulary.example: She began her professional career as an engineer.
Children learn by interacting with others.
Game design requires both creativity and technical skills.

expressions.usage: 어린 나이에 전문적 경험을 시작했음을 강조

expressions.phrase: decided that ... would be my career
expressions.usage: 인생의 중요한 진로 결정 순간을 표현

patterns.pattern: I started [career] and decided at [age] that [X] would be my career after seeing [experience]
patterns.explanation: 경력의 시작과 전환점을 하나의 문장에 압축하는 자서전적 서술 패턴.
examples: I started in marketing and decided at 25 that technology would be my career after seeing its impact.
She started in teaching and decided at 30 that research would be her career after seeing the gaps in education.

drillQuestions.question: I decided that AI _____ be my career.
I decided after _____ how people enjoyed game AI.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 매우 길고 복잡한 구조: (1) started + 분사구문(working), (2) and decided + that절 + after 분사구문(seeing + how절). 시간순으로 경력 시작 → 결심의 순간을 압축적으로 서술한다.

koreanAlignment.en: I started my professional career in game design
working for Bullfrog as a teenager
and decided at around 16 years old
that AI would be my career
after seeing how people enjoyed interacting with game AI
koreanAlignment.ko: 사람들이 게임 AI와 상호작용하는 것을 즐기는 모습을 보고

sentences.original: At DeepMind, we used games as a development platform for AI research.
sentences.translation: 딥마인드에서 우리는 게임을 AI 연구를 위한 개발 플랫폼으로 활용했습니다.

grammarElements.text: as a development platform for AI research
grammarElements.label: 보어 (as — ~로서)

vocabulary.example: The company built a new development platform for its engineers.
The university is known for its cutting-edge research.

expressions.usage: 어떤 것을 특정 목적으로 사용했음을 설명

patterns.pattern: We used [X] as a [platform/tool/method] for [purpose]
patterns.explanation: 연구 또는 개발에서 특정 도구나 방법을 활용했음을 설명하는 학술적 패턴.
examples: We used simulations as a tool for testing new theories.
We used surveys as a method for gathering data.

drillQuestions.question: We used games _____ a development platform.
Games were used as a platform _____ AI research.

sentences.notes: "used X as Y"는 X를 Y의 역할로 활용했다는 의미. "used X for Y"(X를 Y를 위해 사용했다)와 미묘한 차이가 있다.

sentences.original: Games provided the perfect testing ground -- they have clear rules, measurable outcomes, and increasing levels of complexity.
sentences.translation: 게임은 완벽한 테스트 환경을 제공했습니다 — 명확한 규칙, 측정 가능한 결과, 그리고 점점 높아지는 복잡성을 갖추고 있었기 때문입니다.

grammarElements.text: they have clear rules, measurable outcomes, and increasing levels of complexity
grammarElements.label: 부연 설명절 (대시 뒤)

vocabulary.example: The lab served as a testing ground for new technologies.
We need measurable goals for the project.
The complexity of the problem surprised everyone.

expressions.usage: 새로운 아이디어나 기술을 시험해 볼 수 있는 환경
난이도나 복잡성이 점진적으로 증가함을 표현

patterns.pattern: [X] provided the perfect [Y] -- they have [A], [B], and [C]
patterns.explanation: 이유를 3가지 병렬 구조로 나열하며 설명하는 학술적 서술 패턴. 대시(--)로 부연 설명을 연결.
examples: Classrooms provided the perfect learning environment -- they have structure, feedback, and social interaction.
Hospitals provided the perfect training ground -- they have real cases, expert mentors, and advanced equipment.

drillQuestions.question: Games provided the perfect testing _____.
They have clear rules, measurable outcomes, and increasing levels of _____.

sentences.notes: 대시(--)를 사용해 부연 설명을 추가하는 것은 학술 강연에서 흔한 기법. 세 가지 요소(rules, outcomes, complexity)를 병렬로 나열하여 논거의 체계성을 보여준다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 삼항 나열(tricolon) — 세 가지 특성을 나열하여 논증의 완결성을 부여

sentences.original: One of our landmark achievements was AlphaGo, which defeated world champion Lee Sedol in the ancient game of Go.
sentences.translation: 우리의 획기적인 성과 중 하나는 바둑이라는 오래된 게임에서 세계 챔피언 이세돌을 이긴 알파고였습니다.

grammarElements.text: which defeated world champion Lee Sedol in the ancient game of Go
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (계속적 용법)

vocabulary.example: It was a landmark decision by the Supreme Court.
The team defeated their rivals in the final match.
Ancient civilizations built remarkable structures.

expressions.usage: 중요한 업적을 소개할 때 사용하는 겸손한 표현
바둑의 역사적 무게감을 강조하는 표현

patterns.pattern: One of our [adjective] achievements was [X], which [verb] ...
patterns.explanation: 주요 업적을 소개한 뒤, 관계대명사절로 구체적 설명을 추가하는 학술 발표 패턴.
examples: One of our major achievements was the vaccine, which saved millions of lives.
One of our key achievements was the algorithm, which reduced processing time by 90%.

drillQuestions.question: One _____ our landmark achievements was AlphaGo.
AlphaGo _____ world champion Lee Sedol.

sentences.notes: "which"는 비제한적(계속적) 관계대명사로, AlphaGo에 대한 추가 정보를 제공한다. "one of + 복수명사" 구문에서 동사는 단수(was)를 사용함에 주의.

koreanAlignment.en: which defeated world champion Lee Sedol
in the ancient game of Go

sentences.original: The significance of this achievement cannot be overstated -- Go has more possible board positions than there are atoms in the universe, making brute-force computation impossible.
sentences.translation: 이 성과의 중요성은 아무리 강조해도 지나치지 않습니다 — 바둑은 우주의 원자 수보다 더 많은 가능한 판 배치를 가지고 있어, 무차별 대입 계산이 불가능합니다.

grammarElements.text: Go has more possible board positions
grammarElements.label: 주절 (비교급)

grammarElements.text: than there are atoms in the universe
grammarElements.label: 비교절 (than)

vocabulary.example: The significance of this discovery is enormous.
It is hard to overstate the importance of education.
A brute-force approach tries every possible combination.
Modern computers can perform billions of computations per second.

expressions.usage: 무언가의 중요성을 최대한 강조할 때 사용하는 학술적 표현

expressions.phrase: more X than there are Y
expressions.usage: 천문학적 규모를 비유적으로 표현할 때

patterns.pattern: The significance of [X] cannot be overstated -- [explanation]
patterns.explanation: 업적의 중요성을 강조한 뒤 대시로 그 이유를 설명하는 학술적 강조 패턴.
examples: The significance of this treaty cannot be overstated -- it ended decades of conflict.
The significance of clean water cannot be overstated -- millions die each year from waterborne diseases.

drillQuestions.question: The significance of this achievement cannot be _____.
Go has more positions than there _____ atoms in the universe.

sentences.notes: "cannot be overstated"는 이중 부정(can + not + over)으로 강한 긍정을 만드는 수사법. "atoms in the universe"라는 비유는 바둑의 조합적 폭발(combinatorial explosion)을 직관적으로 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Go has more possible board positions
koreanAlignment.ko: 바둑은 더 많은 가능한 판 배치를 가지고 있어

koreanAlignment.en: than there are atoms in the universe

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 과장법(hyperbole) — 우주의 원자 수와 비교하여 바둑의 복잡성을 극적으로 표현

sentences.original: The key moment came with Move 37 in Game 2, a move that no human player would have considered but that turned out to be brilliant.
sentences.translation: 핵심적인 순간은 2국의 37번째 수에서 찾아왔습니다. 어떤 인간 기사도 고려하지 않았을 수이지만, 결과적으로 놀라운 수였습니다.

grammarElements.text: that no human player would have considered
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (가정법 과거완료)

grammarElements.text: but that turned out to be brilliant
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (대조)

vocabulary.example: Have you considered all the options?
The rumor turned out to be true.
She came up with a brilliant solution.

expressions.usage: 전환점이 된 특정 사건을 강조할 때
예상과 다른 결과를 표현할 때

patterns.pattern: [X] that no one would have [past participle] but that turned out to be [adjective]
patterns.explanation: 아무도 예상하지 못한 것이 결과적으로 훌륭했음을 대비하는 패턴. 놀라운 성과를 강조.
examples: It was an idea that no one would have supported but that turned out to be revolutionary.
She chose a path that no one would have recommended but that turned out to be successful.

drillQuestions.question: No human player _____ have considered that move.
The move turned _____ to be brilliant.

sentences.notes: 두 개의 that 관계대명사절이 but으로 연결된 구조. 첫 번째 that절은 가정법 과거완료(would have considered)로 "인간이라면 생각하지 않았을"이라는 비현실을, 두 번째 that절은 실제 결과를 대비.

koreanAlignment.en: a move that no human player would have considered
koreanAlignment.ko: 어떤 인간 기사도 고려하지 않았을 수

koreanAlignment.en: but that turned out to be brilliant

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 극적 반전(dramatic reversal) — 불가능해 보였던 수가 실제로는 천재적이었다는 반전

sentences.original: This demonstrated that neural networks could discover strategies that go beyond human intuition, efficiently navigating complex decision trees in ways that surprised even the greatest experts.
sentences.translation: 이것은 신경망이 인간의 직관을 넘어서는 전략을 발견할 수 있음을 보여주었습니다. 최고의 전문가들조차 놀라게 하는 방식으로 복잡한 의사결정 트리를 효율적으로 탐색한 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: that neural networks could discover strategies
grammarElements.label: that절 (목적절)

grammarElements.text: in ways that surprised even the greatest experts
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 + 관계대명사절

vocabulary.example: The neural network learned to recognize faces.
She relied on her intuition to make the decision.
The robot can navigate complex terrain.
Decision trees are a common tool in machine learning.

expressions.usage: AI가 인간의 한계를 초월했음을 표현
"even"으로 놀라움의 범위를 극대화

patterns.pattern: This demonstrated that [X] could [verb] ... in ways that surprised even [Y]
patterns.explanation: 실험 결과가 예상을 뛰어넘었음을 보여주는 학술적 결론 패턴.
examples: This demonstrated that AI could compose music in ways that surprised even professional musicians.
This demonstrated that simple algorithms could solve problems in ways that surprised even seasoned engineers.

drillQuestions.question: This demonstrated _____ neural networks could discover new strategies.
Strategies that go _____ human intuition.

sentences.notes: 이 문장에는 that이 3번 등장: (1) demonstrated that (접속사), (2) strategies that go beyond (관계대명사), (3) ways that surprised (관계대명사). 분사구문(navigating)은 동시 진행 상황을 설명. 기술적 용어(neural network, decision tree)가 포함된 고난도 문장.

koreanAlignment.en: in ways that surprised even the greatest experts

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 점층법(climax) — "even the greatest experts"로 놀라움의 정도를 점진적으로 높임

sentences.original: Straight after AlphaGo, I felt ready to tackle scientific problems, and protein folding was the challenge I had been thinking about for almost 20 years.
sentences.translation: 알파고 직후, 저는 과학적 문제에 도전할 준비가 되었다고 느꼈고, 단백질 접힘은 거의 20년간 생각해 온 과제였습니다.

grammarElements.text: and protein folding was the challenge
grammarElements.label: 등위절

grammarElements.text: I had been thinking about for almost 20 years
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (과거완료 진행)

vocabulary.example: I went home straight after work.
We need to tackle climate change urgently.
Protein folding determines how proteins function.

expressions.usage: 새로운 도전에 나설 준비가 된 심리적 상태를 표현
과거완료 진행형으로 오랜 기간의 숙고를 강조

patterns.pattern: After [achievement], I felt ready to [next challenge], and [X] was [something] I had been [verb]-ing about for [time]
patterns.explanation: 하나의 성과 뒤에 다음 목표를 제시하고, 그것이 오래된 꿈이었음을 밝히는 자서전적 전환 패턴.
examples: After finishing college, I felt ready to start my own business, and technology was the field I had been dreaming about for years.

drillQuestions.question: _____ after AlphaGo, I felt ready to tackle scientific problems.
Protein folding was the challenge I _____ been thinking about.

sentences.notes: "had been thinking about"는 과거완료 진행형으로, 과거의 한 시점(AlphaGo 이후)까지 계속된 사고 과정을 강조한다. "for almost 20 years"는 그 기간의 길이를 통해 이 과제에 대한 깊은 관심을 드러낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: I felt ready to tackle scientific problems
koreanAlignment.ko: 과학적 문제에 도전할 준비가 되었다고 느꼈고

koreanAlignment.en: and protein folding was the challenge
I had been thinking about for almost 20 years

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 전환(transition) — 게임 AI에서 과학적 AI로의 연구 전환을 개인적 서사로 연결

sentences.original: The protein folding problem -- predicting a protein's three-dimensional structure from its amino acid sequence -- had been one of the grand challenges of biology for over 50 years.
sentences.translation: 단백질 접힘 문제 — 아미노산 서열로부터 단백질의 3차원 구조를 예측하는 것 — 는 50년 이상 생물학의 거대한 도전 과제 중 하나였습니다.

grammarElements.text: predicting a protein's three-dimensional structure from its amino acid sequence
grammarElements.label: 삽입구 (동격 — 대시로 구분)

grammarElements.text: one of the grand challenges of biology
grammarElements.label: 보어

vocabulary.example: The model shows a three-dimensional view of the building.
There are 20 standard amino acids in proteins.
The DNA sequence was analyzed in the lab.
Climate change is one of the grand challenges of our time.

expressions.phrase: one of the grand challenges of
expressions.usage: 학문 분야의 핵심 난제를 소개할 때
문제의 오랜 역사와 난이도를 강조

patterns.pattern: The [X] problem -- [definition] -- had been one of the grand challenges of [field] for over [time]
patterns.explanation: 전문 용어를 대시 사이에 정의를 삽입하여 설명하고, 문제의 역사적 무게를 강조하는 학술 발표 패턴.
examples: The consciousness problem -- explaining how subjective experience arises from brain activity -- had been one of the grand challenges of neuroscience for over a century.

drillQuestions.question: The protein folding problem _____ been one of the grand challenges.
Predicting a protein's structure from its amino acid _____.

sentences.notes: 대시(--)로 삽입된 "predicting ... sequence" 부분은 동격(apposition)으로, protein folding problem을 정의한다. 과거완료(had been)는 이 문제가 AlphaFold 이전부터 존재했음을 시간적으로 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: had been one of the grand challenges of biology
koreanAlignment.ko: 생물학의 거대한 도전 과제 중 하나였습니다

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 삽입 정의(parenthetical definition) — 전문 용어를 대시 사이에 정의하여 비전문가 청중도 이해할 수 있게 배려

sentences.original: Proteins are the molecular machines of life, responsible for virtually every function in every living cell.
sentences.translation: 단백질은 생명의 분자 기계로, 모든 살아있는 세포의 거의 모든 기능을 담당합니다.

grammarElements.text: responsible for virtually every function in every living cell
grammarElements.label: 형용사구 (후치 수식)

vocabulary.example: Molecular biology studies the structure of cells.
Virtually all students passed the exam.
Each organ has a specific function in the body.

expressions.usage: 단백질을 기계에 비유하여 그 역할과 정밀함을 강조하는 은유
특정 기능이나 역할을 맡고 있음을 표현

patterns.pattern: [X] are the [metaphor], responsible for virtually every [Y]
patterns.explanation: 과학적 개념을 은유로 정의한 뒤, 그 보편적 중요성을 "virtually every"로 강조하는 패턴.
examples: Enzymes are the catalysts of biology, responsible for virtually every chemical reaction in the body.
Teachers are the architects of the future, responsible for virtually every aspect of student development.

drillQuestions.question: Proteins are the molecular _____ of life.
Proteins are responsible _____ virtually every function.

sentences.notes: "the molecular machines of life"는 강력한 은유(metaphor)로, 단백질의 정교하고 기계적인 작동 방식을 한 문구로 압축. "virtually every"는 "거의 모든"이라는 포괄적 의미로 단백질의 중요성을 극대화.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 은유(metaphor) — 단백질을 "생명의 분자 기계"로 비유하여 과학적 개념을 직관적으로 전달

sentences.original: Understanding their structure is essential to understanding how they work, which in turn is essential for developing new medicines and understanding disease.
sentences.translation: 단백질의 구조를 이해하는 것은 단백질이 어떻게 작동하는지를 이해하는 데 필수적이며, 이는 결국 새로운 의약품 개발과 질병 이해에 필수적입니다.

grammarElements.text: essential to understanding how they work
grammarElements.label: 보어 (essential to)

grammarElements.text: which in turn is essential for developing new medicines and understanding disease
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (계속적 용법)

vocabulary.example: Water is essential for life.
Better education leads to better jobs, which in turn leads to economic growth.
New medicines are being developed to fight cancer.

expressions.usage: 핵심적 필요성을 강조
인과의 연쇄를 표현할 때. A → B → C 구조

patterns.pattern: Understanding [X] is essential to understanding [Y], which in turn is essential for [Z]
patterns.explanation: 연쇄적 논리(A는 B에 필수, B는 C에 필수)를 표현하는 학술적 인과 패턴.
examples: Understanding grammar is essential to understanding literature, which in turn is essential for appreciating culture.
Understanding data is essential to understanding trends, which in turn is essential for making informed decisions.

drillQuestions.question: Understanding their structure is essential _____ understanding how they work.
This is essential for developing new medicines, which _____ turn helps patients.

sentences.notes: "essential to"와 "essential for"가 모두 사용됨: "essential to + 동명사"(이해에 필수), "essential for + 동명사"(개발에 필수). 미묘한 차이가 있지만, 둘 다 "필수적인"이라는 의미. "which in turn"은 인과의 연쇄를 만드는 학술적 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: is essential to understanding how they work
which in turn is essential for

sentences.original: We presented AlphaFold2 in 2020, and it represented a fundamental breakthrough.
sentences.translation: 우리는 2020년에 알파폴드2를 발표했으며, 이것은 근본적인 돌파구를 나타냈습니다.

grammarElements.text: and it represented a fundamental breakthrough
grammarElements.label: 등위절

vocabulary.example: The team presented their findings at the conference.
There is a fundamental difference between the two approaches.
Scientists made a breakthrough in cancer research.

expressions.usage: 혁신적 성과의 의미를 학술적으로 표현

patterns.pattern: We presented [X] in [year], and it represented a [adjective] breakthrough
patterns.explanation: 연구 결과 발표와 그 의의를 간결하게 서술하는 학술 발표 패턴.
examples: We presented the new model in 2023, and it represented a significant breakthrough.
The team presented their findings in 2019, and it represented a major breakthrough.

drillQuestions.question: We _____ AlphaFold2 in 2020.
It represented a _____ breakthrough.

sentences.notes: "present"는 "선물"이 아닌 "발표하다"의 의미. "represented"는 단순히 "~였다"가 아닌 "~을 나타냈다/상징했다"라는 학술적 동사. 짧고 명확한 문장으로 AlphaFold2의 중요성을 선언.

koreanAlignment.en: and it represented a fundamental breakthrough

sentences.original: The system could predict protein structures with accuracy comparable to experimental methods, but in minutes rather than the months or years required by traditional techniques like X-ray crystallography or cryo-electron microscopy.
sentences.translation: 이 시스템은 실험적 방법에 필적하는 정확도로 단백질 구조를 예측할 수 있었지만, X선 결정학이나 극저온 전자 현미경과 같은 전통적 기법에 필요한 수개월 또는 수년이 아닌 단 몇 분 만에 가능했습니다.

grammarElements.text: with accuracy comparable to experimental methods
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (방식)

grammarElements.text: rather than the months or years
grammarElements.label: 비교구 (rather than)

grammarElements.text: required by traditional techniques like X-ray crystallography or cryo-electron microscopy
grammarElements.label: 과거분사구 (수식)

vocabulary.example: The quality is comparable to that of more expensive brands.
X-ray crystallography reveals atomic structures.
Cryo-electron microscopy allows scientists to see molecules at near-atomic resolution.
The model achieved remarkable accuracy.

expressions.usage: 성능 비교를 학술적으로 표현할 때

expressions.phrase: in minutes rather than months or years
expressions.meaning: 몇 분 만에, 수개월이나 수년이 아닌
expressions.usage: 시간 단축의 극적 효과를 대비로 표현

patterns.pattern: [System] could [verb] with accuracy comparable to [X], but in [short time] rather than [long time] required by [traditional method]
patterns.explanation: AI 시스템의 성능을 기존 방법과 비교하며, 시간 단축을 극적으로 대비하는 학술적 성과 발표 패턴.
examples: The AI could diagnose with accuracy comparable to specialists, but in seconds rather than the hours required by traditional screening.

drillQuestions.question: The system could predict structures with accuracy _____ to experimental methods.
It could do it in minutes _____ than months or years.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 강연에서 가장 복잡한 구조 중 하나. 기술적 전문 용어(crystallography, cryo-electron microscopy)가 포함되어 있으며, "minutes vs months/years"의 극적 대비가 AlphaFold2의 혁신성을 부각. "required by"는 수동태로 전통 기법에 의해 요구되는 시간을 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: The system could predict protein structures
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 시스템은 단백질 구조를 예측할 수 있었지만

koreanAlignment.en: with accuracy comparable to experimental methods
rather than the months or years
like X-ray crystallography or cryo-electron microscopy
koreanAlignment.ko: X선 결정학이나 극저온 전자 현미경과 같은

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 극적 대비(dramatic contrast) — "minutes vs months/years"로 혁신의 규모를 체감시킴

sentences.original: AlphaFold has since been used by more than two million researchers worldwide for work ranging from enzyme design to drug discovery.
sentences.translation: 알파폴드는 이후 전 세계 200만 명 이상의 연구자들이 효소 설계부터 신약 발견에 이르는 연구에 활용해 왔습니다.

grammarElements.text: by more than two million researchers worldwide
grammarElements.label: 행위자 (by + 명사구)

grammarElements.text: for work ranging from enzyme design to drug discovery
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (목적)

vocabulary.example: Enzymes speed up chemical reactions in the body.
AI is transforming the field of drug discovery.
The students ranged from beginners to advanced.

expressions.usage: 발표 이후의 영향력을 현재완료로 표현
넓은 범위의 응용 분야를 표현

patterns.pattern: [X] has since been used by [number] [people] worldwide for work ranging from [A] to [B]
patterns.explanation: 연구 도구의 광범위한 영향력과 채택을 수치와 범위로 표현하는 학술적 패턴.
examples: The database has since been used by thousands of scientists worldwide for work ranging from genetics to ecology.

drillQuestions.question: AlphaFold has _____ been used by millions of researchers.
Work _____ from enzyme design to drug discovery.

sentences.notes: "has since been used"에서 "since"는 "그 이후로"라는 부사로, 현재완료 수동태와 결합. "two million researchers"라는 구체적 수치가 AlphaFold의 영향력을 객관적으로 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: by more than two million researchers worldwide
koreanAlignment.ko: 전 세계 200만 명 이상의 연구자들에 의해

koreanAlignment.en: for work ranging from enzyme design

sentences.original: We released predicted structures for virtually all 200 million known proteins, creating a comprehensive database that has transformed the field of structural biology.
sentences.translation: 우리는 알려진 거의 모든 2억 개의 단백질에 대한 예측 구조를 공개하여, 구조생물학 분야를 변혁시킨 종합적인 데이터베이스를 만들었습니다.

grammarElements.text: for virtually all 200 million known proteins
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (대상)

grammarElements.text: that has transformed the field of structural biology
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (현재완료)

vocabulary.example: The company released the data to the public.
The report provides a comprehensive overview.
Structural biology studies the molecular structure of biological macromolecules.
Technology has transformed the way we communicate.

expressions.usage: 극히 높은 비율의 포함을 표현
혁신적 영향을 학술적으로 표현

patterns.pattern: We released [X] for virtually all [number] [Y], creating a [adjective] [Z] that has transformed [field]
patterns.explanation: 데이터 공개의 규모와 그 혁신적 영향을 하나의 문장에 담는 학술적 성과 서술 패턴.
examples: We released the toolkit for virtually all programming languages, creating a universal framework that has transformed software development.

drillQuestions.question: We released structures for _____ all 200 million known proteins.
The database has _____ the field of structural biology.

sentences.notes: "200 million"이라는 숫자가 프로젝트의 압도적 규모를 보여준다. 분사구문(creating)은 공개의 결과를 표현하며, 관계대명사절(that has transformed)은 현재까지 이어지는 영향을 강조.

koreanAlignment.en: for virtually all 200 million known proteins
koreanAlignment.ko: 알려진 거의 모든 2억 개의 단백질에 대한

koreanAlignment.en: that has transformed the field of structural biology

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 수치적 증거(numerical evidence) — "200 million"이라는 압도적 수치로 성과의 규모를 객관적으로 제시

sentences.original: The success of AlphaFold demonstrates the enormous potential of AI as a tool for scientific discovery.
sentences.translation: 알파폴드의 성공은 과학적 발견을 위한 도구로서의 AI의 거대한 잠재력을 보여줍니다.

grammarElements.text: as a tool for scientific discovery
grammarElements.label: 보어 (as — ~로서)

vocabulary.example: The experiment demonstrates a key principle.
The project requires an enormous amount of funding.
The technology has great potential for growth.

expressions.usage: 성과를 통해 미래 가능성을 논증할 때
AI의 역할을 도구적 관점에서 정의

patterns.pattern: The success of [X] demonstrates the [adjective] potential of [Y] as a tool for [Z]
patterns.explanation: 구체적 성과에서 일반적 잠재력으로 논의를 확장하는 학술적 결론 패턴.
examples: The success of GPS demonstrates the enormous potential of satellite technology as a tool for navigation.
The success of CRISPR demonstrates the enormous potential of gene editing as a tool for medicine.

drillQuestions.question: The success of AlphaFold _____ the enormous potential of AI.
AI serves _____ a tool for scientific discovery.

sentences.notes: 현재 시제(demonstrates)를 사용하여 AlphaFold의 성공이 현재에도 유효한 증거임을 강조. 이 문장은 구체적 사례(AlphaFold)에서 일반적 원칙(AI의 잠재력)으로 논의를 전환하는 전환점.

koreanAlignment.en: as a tool for scientific discovery

sentences.original: By combining deep learning with scientific domain knowledge, we can accelerate research across many fields -- from materials science to drug development.
sentences.translation: 딥러닝과 과학적 도메인 지식을 결합함으로써, 재료 과학에서 신약 개발에 이르기까지 많은 분야의 연구를 가속화할 수 있습니다.

grammarElements.text: By combining deep learning with scientific domain knowledge
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (방법 — by + 동명사)

grammarElements.text: from materials science to drug development
grammarElements.label: 범위 부사구 (from A to B)

vocabulary.example: Deep learning has revolutionized image recognition.
Domain knowledge is crucial for solving industry-specific problems.
The new technology could accelerate the pace of discovery.
Materials science is essential for developing new technologies.

expressions.usage: 방법론을 설명할 때 by + 동명사 구문 사용

expressions.phrase: from materials science to drug development
expressions.usage: 폭넓은 적용 범위를 보여주는 표현

patterns.pattern: By combining [X] with [Y], we can accelerate [Z] across many fields -- from [A] to [B]
patterns.explanation: 방법론(결합)을 제시하고, 그 영향 범위를 "from A to B"로 확장하는 비전 제시 패턴.
examples: By combining AI with genomics, we can accelerate disease diagnosis across many areas -- from rare diseases to common infections.

drillQuestions.question: _____ combining deep learning with domain knowledge, we can accelerate research.
We can _____ research across many fields.

sentences.notes: "By + 동명사"는 방법을 설명하는 학술적 구문. "from A to B" 패턴이 두 번 사용(combining A with B, from materials science to drug development)되어 결합과 범위를 동시에 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: By combining deep learning with scientific domain knowledge
from materials science to drug development

sentences.original: At Isomorphic Labs, we are now applying these same principles to drug discovery, working to design new medicines at digital speed.
sentences.translation: 아이소모르픽 랩스에서 우리는 지금 이 동일한 원칙들을 신약 발견에 적용하고 있으며, 디지털 속도로 새로운 의약품을 설계하기 위해 노력하고 있습니다.

grammarElements.text: working to design new medicines at digital speed
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (동시 진행)

vocabulary.example: The two structures are isomorphic.
The same principles apply in different contexts.
AI enables us to work at digital speed.

expressions.usage: 성공한 방법론을 새로운 분야에 확장할 때
컴퓨터에 의한 초고속 작업을 비유적으로 표현

patterns.pattern: At [Company], we are now applying [principles] to [new field], working to [goal]
patterns.explanation: 현재 진행 중인 신규 사업과 목표를 소개하는 기업 발표 패턴.
examples: At our research center, we are now applying machine learning to climate modeling, working to predict extreme weather events.

drillQuestions.question: We are now _____ these same principles to drug discovery.
We are working to design new medicines at digital _____.

sentences.notes: "at digital speed"는 하사비스가 자주 사용하는 표현으로, AI에 의한 연구 속도의 혁명을 함축적으로 전달한다. "Isomorphic"은 수학 용어로 "같은 형태의"라는 뜻으로, 생물학과 AI의 구조적 유사성을 암시하는 회사명.

koreanAlignment.en: we are now applying these same principles

koreanAlignment.ko: 새로운 의약품을 설계하기 위해 노력하고 있습니다

sentences.original: The goal is to use AI not just to understand existing biological structures, but to design entirely new molecules with specific therapeutic properties.
sentences.translation: 목표는 AI를 단순히 기존의 생물학적 구조를 이해하는 데만 사용하는 것이 아니라, 특정 치료적 특성을 가진 완전히 새로운 분자를 설계하는 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: not just to understand existing biological structures
grammarElements.label: 부정 + to부정사 (제한)

grammarElements.text: but to design entirely new molecules with specific therapeutic properties
grammarElements.label: 대조 to부정사 (확장된 목표)

vocabulary.example: The drug has significant therapeutic benefits.
Water is a molecule made of hydrogen and oxygen.
The two approaches are entirely different.

expressions.usage: 더 야심찬 목표를 대비로 제시
약물 설계에서 원하는 효능을 표현

patterns.pattern: The goal is to [verb] not just to [A], but to [B]
patterns.explanation: 제한적 목표(A)를 넘어 더 큰 비전(B)을 제시하는 비전 선언 패턴.
examples: The goal is to use technology not just to automate tasks, but to augment human creativity.
The goal is to apply research not just to solve current problems, but to prevent future ones.

drillQuestions.question: The goal is to use AI not _____ to understand structures.
We aim to design molecules with specific _____ properties.

sentences.notes: "not just A, but B" 구문이 to부정사와 결합하여 목표의 확장을 표현. "understand"(이해)에서 "design"(설계)으로의 전환은 AI의 역할이 수동적 분석에서 능동적 창조로 진화했음을 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: The goal is to use AI
not just to understand existing biological structures
koreanAlignment.ko: 기존의 생물학적 구조를 이해하는 데만이 아니라

koreanAlignment.en: but to design entirely new molecules

pronunciationNotes: therapeutic: /ˌθerəˈpjuːtɪk/ — "쎄러퓨틱", th는 /θ/ 발음, 강세는 세 번째 음절
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 대조적 확장(contrastive expansion) — "not just A, but B"로 목표의 야심을 드라마틱하게 확장

sentences.original: The age of digital biology is here, and AI will be the engine that drives the next era of scientific breakthroughs.
sentences.translation: 디지털 생물학의 시대가 도래했으며, AI는 다음 시대의 과학적 돌파구를 이끄는 엔진이 될 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: that drives the next era of scientific breakthroughs
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (engine 수식)

vocabulary.example: We are entering a new era of technology.
Innovation is the engine of economic growth.
Recent breakthroughs in AI have been remarkable.
Digital biology uses computational tools to study living systems.

expressions.phrase: the age of X is here
expressions.usage: 새로운 시대의 시작을 선언하는 극적인 표현
핵심적인 추진력을 은유적으로 표현

patterns.pattern: The age of [X] is here, and [Y] will be the engine that drives [Z]
patterns.explanation: 새로운 시대의 도래를 선언하고, 그 핵심 동력을 은유적으로 제시하는 강연 마무리 패턴.
examples: The age of renewable energy is here, and innovation will be the engine that drives the transition.
The age of personalized medicine is here, and genomics will be the engine that drives treatment.

drillQuestions.question: The age of digital biology _____ here.
AI will be the engine that _____ the next era.

sentences.notes: 강연의 마지막 문장으로, 선언적이고 비전적인 톤을 사용. "is here"는 현재 시제로 미래가 이미 시작되었음을 선언하고, "will be"는 미래의 확신을 표현. "engine"은 AI를 추진력의 은유로 사용하여 역동성을 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The age of digital biology is here
and AI will be the engine

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 선언적 종결(declarative closing) — "The age is here"로 새 시대의 도래를 선언하며 강연을 마무리하는 수사법
